expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Ok, I've read everything you sent — here's what I see"]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"You've actually got a solid trip here"},"children":["Five pieces of material covering a Tanzania trip (Aug 3–20, 2026) for you and Athina Mari: Cairo → Arusha → Ngorongoro → Serengeti → Zanzibar. The notes file maps the whole itinerary, the spreadsheet breaks down every cost, the crater PDF has the official park fees, the voice memo captured on-the-ground details from crater day, and the Stone Town photo is a gorgeous shot of that classic Zanzibari courtyard light."]},{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":3},"children":["The things I noticed that you'll want at your fingertips"]},{"type":"List","props":{"items":["The ranger tip Emmanuel mentioned — TZS 5,000 cash at the crater gate, separate from the park fees. Your notes don't have it, and nobody told you before. That's exactly the kind of thing you'll forget at the moment it matters.","The bracelet purchase is hazy — $20 or $25, paid ~TZS 35,000. A small thing now, but if you're tracking every cost later it's a gap.","The PDF shows the official Ngorongoro fees — you can cross-check those against what Richard quotes for the safari balance.","Your spreadsheet is the cost backbone — flights, safari, hotels, insurance, visas — but it's one file on your phone, not something you can glance at quickly."]},"children":[]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["You've got rules from the crater PDF, a note from Emmanuel you dictated to yourself, a cost spreadsheet, itinerary notes, and a memory from Stone Town — spread across five formats. On your phone, in the moment, that's going to be impossible to piece together."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":[{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["Want me to pull all of this into a single app you can open on your phone?"]}," I'd build it right here in this project — your costs in a table, your itinerary with the key details per stop, the crater fee rules, the bracelet purchase noted, and the ranger tip called out so you see it before safari day. You'd open one link and everything would be there, searchable, no digging through files."]}]}

The trip combines cultural sightseeing in Cairo, a group camping safari through Tarangire, Lake Manyara, and Ngorongoro Crate…
